title: season — validation rules
description: Seven custom Laravel validation rules published by SeasonServiceProvider::boot() so any FormRequest / Data class can validate Season-adjacent fields. Named after their intent so they read cleanly at the composition site.

rules.name: valid_hex_color
rules.description: Ensures the value is a hex color literal (`#RRGGBB` or `#RGB`, case-insensitive). Used on Season.primary_color. Same-shape rule as the sibling in branch.rules — both share the underlying HexColorValidator utility from foundation.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a hex color like #RRGGBB or #RGB.

rules.name: valid_date_range
rules.description: Ensures `end_date > start_date` with an optional minimum + maximum span in days. Used at the Data-class layer (SeasonCreateData) to check start_date + end_date fields together. Field-level rule; must be attached to the composite object, not a single field.

params.name: min_days
params.description: Minimum span in days (default 1 — a Season must span at least one day).

params.name: max_days
params.description: Maximum span in days (default 3653 = 10 years — an upper bound to catch mis-parsed dates).

rules.default_message: The :attribute's date range must be between :min_days and :max_days days.

rules.name: valid_registration_window
rules.description: Composite rule ensuring the registration timestamps form a coherent window: `registration_opens_at < registration_closes_at`, `registration_closes_at <= start_date + max_grace_days` (default 30), `late_registration_ends_at > registration_closes_at` (when set), `late_registration_ends_at <= end_date`. Field-level rule attached to the SeasonCreateData / SeasonUpdateData class-level.
rules.default_message: The registration window is invalid: opens must precede closes, closes must land within :max_grace_days of start, and the late window (when set) must open after closes and end by end_date.

rules.name: valid_playoff_window
rules.description: Composite rule ensuring the playoff timestamps are internally consistent: when `has_playoffs=true`, `playoff_starts_at` MUST be set + fall within [start_date, end_date]; `playoff_ends_at` (when set) MUST fall in [playoff_starts_at, end_date]. Field-level rule.
rules.default_message: The playoff window is invalid: has_playoffs requires playoff_starts_at, and both playoff dates must fall within the season's date range.

rules.name: valid_season_scoping
rules.description: Composite rule ensuring the scoping tuple (tenant_id, organization_id?, branch_id?) is coherent: when branch_id is set, organization_id must be set; branch.organization_id must match organization_id; branch.tenant_id + organization.tenant_id must match tenant_id. Field-level rule.
rules.default_message: The scoping is invalid: a branch-scoped season must include the branch's organization, and both must belong to the same tenant.

rules.name: one_current_per_scope_tuple
rules.description: Ensures the caller isn't attempting to set is_current=true when another Season with the same (tenant_id, organization_id, branch_id, sport_key) tuple already has is_current=true — the SAME rule the observer enforces, exposed as a validation rule so form-level errors surface before the transaction (better UX). The observer stays as the enforcement floor.
rules.default_message: Another Season in this scope tuple is already marked current. Setting this Season current will demote the other automatically — confirm the operation on the review screen.

rules.name: valid_sport_key
rules.description: Ensures sport_key matches `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$` (lowercase, alphanumeric + underscore, starts with a letter). Wave 3a treats sport_key as a free-text discriminator with format validation only. Wave 3c will introduce a sport registry + this rule will additionally check the key against `sports.is_active=true`.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must match the pattern lowercase-letter followed by lowercase-alphanumeric-or-underscore (e.g. football, basketball, swimming_freestyle).
